input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works in .NET 10. This is a known change — Android itself deprecated `adjustResize` in API 30+, and .NET MAUI has been evolving its handling of this.

Let me think about what I know:

In .NET MAUI / .NET 9 and earlier, you could use:
```csharp
App.Current.On<Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.Android>()
    .UseWindowSoftInputModeAdjust(WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize);
```

But in .NET 10 (which targets newer Android APIs), `adjustResize` is deprecated by Android. The recommended approach is to use `edge-to-edge` display and handle keyboard insets properly. MAUI adopted Android's edge-to-edge mode by default in .NET 10, which means `adjustResize` no longer works — instead, I need to handle `KeyboardInsets` or `WindowInsets` properly to account for the keyboard's presence.

For .NET 10 specifically, Android enforces edge-to-edge by default starting with Android 15, so `adjustResize` is deprecated. The proper solution involves using the `WindowInsets` API through `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener`, or in MAUI's case, adding padding or margins when the keyboard appears. There's also `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which was introduced in .NET 8 and handles this automatically. MAUI provides built-in keyboard handling through `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which automatically scrolls focused elements into view, but there are a few practical approaches depending on the situation. I can either wrap content in a `ScrollView`, manually adjust padding based on keyboard insets on Android, or leverage .NET 10's improved edge-to-edge support with proper inset handling. The key thing is that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` is now obsolete since Android moved away from the `adjustResize` approach in favor of the inset system.

For a chat screen specifically where you want the layout to compress rather than scroll, the best strategy is to listen for keyboard inset changes and adjust the bottom padding of your root layout accordingly. I'm setting up a layout structure...

Writing platform-specific code... Finishing the listener implementation...

Now I'm thinking through the actual problem more carefully. Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge rendering by default, which means the traditional `adjustResize` approach doesn't work anymore. .NET MAUI in .NET 10 adopted this edge-to-edge behavior, so the solution is to properly handle `WindowInsets` callbacks to adjust the page padding when the IME appears, accounting for the keyboard height relative to the system bars.
